title: ptilostemon chamaepeuce subsp. cyprius
intro: Ptilostemon chamaepeuce subsp. cyprius, also known as Cypriot rock rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Cyprus, for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Ptilostemon chamaepeuce subsp. cyprius include reducing inflammation and fighting infections, making it useful for treating conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.
